steps.title: Identify coefficients from the standard form
Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Apply the quadratic formula by substituting the coefficients:
  • Numerator: $-b = -7$
  • Denominator: $2a = 2(5) = 10$
  • Discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 7^2 - 4(5)(-6) = 49 - (-120) = 49 + 120$
Therefore, the solution is $x = \frac{-7 \pm \sqrt{49 + 120}}{10}$. That confirms A is correct.
